title: Vedic Epics: Fact or Fiction? | HG Amitasana Dasa
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So here Prabhupada is mentioning that all these auspicious narrations about the Lord,
his activities, they are of transcendental nature. These are all not fictitious narrations.
Many times people, when they narrate some stories from Ramayana, Mahabharata, they call it Indian mythology.
Many who truly study the Vedas or even Ramayana, Mahabharata, actually may have an element of faithlessness
where they believe that these are all mythology stories. No doubt they have important lessons,
they have wisdom. So Srila Prabhupada very nicely, very faithfully, very diligently presented
Bhaktivedanta Purports and instilled in us the conviction that these are all not fictitious stories.
Bhagavatam is actually talking about the things which have happened in the past, actually happened.
May not be in this planet earth, may not be in this yuga, because what we know of this world,
what we know of ourselves is very small, insignificant portion of the reality.
So if a person is humble, understands his true position, understands that life exists in all the planets,
there are many many brahmandas, this universe is not all in all.
There is a spiritual world, there is a material world.
Understanding we get is our universe is just like a small mustard seed in a bag of mustard seeds.
And these are all material universes, which is one fourth of the Lord's creation.
So we are in that universe, one small insignificant, in that universe we have billions of galaxies.
And the Milky Way, one galaxy in which we are part of, it's huge.
Millions of stars, each of them having family of planets.
So how small insignificant we are, how arrogant we could be to say that all these are all, life exists only here.
We know about this world.
We hardly know about our body, how it functions.
Yesterday I was studying about, it was described in the camp which we attended, about the human digestive system.
It's so complex, so complex.
The way, the moment we put the food in the mouth, till it finally we evacuate.
It's so complex, so many, you know, processes happening in different parts of the digestive system.
So it's not just come on its own.
There is Lord, He is a reality.
He has designed this world for a purpose, He has designed us, we have come in this world for a purpose.
So Prabhupada mentions that when the Lord comes in this material world, He enacts various pastimes.
A narrative history which is transcendental as Himself.
So when we hear about the Lord and His pastimes and His activities, we also rise above the modes.
Because the nature of these activities are transcendental.
They have the potency, they have the power to purify us and uplift us.
We are soaked, we are entrapped in the lower modes, the modes of material nature which is binding us to this material world.
So here Prabhupada mentions that every living entity of a superior quality as a spirit soul.
And nothing material can be auspicious for Him.
In this material world we have our own concoction, this is auspicious, this is inauspicious, this is good, this is bad.
So Prabhupada says, we being spirit soul, nothing material can be auspicious.
So only those things which are of transcendental nature, the Lord, His activities, His holy name, His pastimes, the deities, prasadam.
All these are all actually auspicious for the living entity.
We are intelligent persons so therefore hear personally and cause others to hear the descriptive narrations of Lord's activities.
For that will destroy the pangs of material existence.
Out of His causeless mercy only the Lord comes to this earth and leave behind His merciful activities so that the devotees may derive transcendental benefit.
